desktop post-delivery callback intents are persisted as `hermes_post_delivery_callback_registry_desktop_v1`, chain same-session/same-generation callbacks, reject stale-generation registrations, pop one-shot callbacks at session-finished delivery, execute them through the existing shell-hook runner with isolated failure records, and persist callback fire history under `platforms/post_delivery_callbacks.json`; Slack local MEDIA attachments use `files.getUploadURLExternal` and `files.completeUploadExternal`; Email local MEDIA attachments are sent as `multipart/mixed` SMTP attachments; SMS/Twilio sends public `MEDIA:https://...` URLs as repeated `MediaUrl` form fields while rejecting local files; DingTalk webhook routing renders remote `MEDIA:https://...` image URLs as markdown images while rejecting local files, matching Hermes session-webhook limitations; WhatsApp local MEDIA attachments are forwarded to the Hermes bridge-compatible `/send-media` endpoint with image/video/audio/document type mapping; QQBot local MEDIA attachments use C2C/group `/files` upload plus `msg_type: 7` media messages for small files and Hermes-style chunked upload (`upload_prepare`, COS part `PUT`, `upload_part_finish`, complete via `/files` `upload_id`) for large local files; `/qqbot onboard` implements Hermes-style `create_bind_task`/`poll_bind_result` QR onboarding and AES-256-GCM `bot_encrypt_secret` decryption without printing secrets; QQBot inline keyboard payloads support approval/update-confirm buttons, button-data parsing, `INTERACTION_CREATE` ACK via `PUT /interactions/{id}`, authorized approval routing, background approval resume for allow choices, deny routing, allow-once approval replay to internal tools, channel metadata in adapter state, and `.update_response` writes for update prompts; Feishu/Lark Drive comment tools cover document read, comment/reply listing, reply, whole-document comment, and Hermes-style Drive v2 reply reaction add/delete via `feishu_drive_update_comment_reaction`; Feishu drive comment events apply Hermes-style `feishu_comment_rules.json` exact/wildcard/top/default resolution plus `allowlist`/`pairing` policies and `feishu_comment_pairing.json` approved users before dispatch; when `feishu.commentAutomation.enabled` / `comment_automation.enabled` is configured, comment inbound prompts switch to Hermes-style agent tool orchestration requiring OK reaction, context inspection, Feishu doc read as needed, reply/add-comment routing, and add-comment fallback guidance; `/platforms feishu-comment` provides Hermes-style rules status, access check, and pairing list/add/remove/clear management for those files; WeCom raw callback media enrichment caches base64 payloads and downloads URL attachments with Hermes-style AES-256-CBC decryption; Yuanbao raw Tencent TIM callbacks parse PascalCase/snake_case message fields, group/user identity, TIMText/TIMImage/TIMFile/TIMSound/TIMVideo/TIMCustom/TIMFace text placeholders, media resource metadata, and optional `resourceId` resolution via `/api/resource/v1/download` before local cache; receive runtime supports configured gateway platforms including ntfy native streaming JSON events, SimpleX daemon WebSocket events/file receive, IRC TCP/TLS socket events, Discord adapter, MS Graph webhook parsing, Feishu drive comment event parsing/access gating/agent-tool orchestration prompts, and QQBot onboarding diagnostics; completed desktop chat turns now fire session lifecycle hooks after local assistant delivery with `hermes_post_delivery_callback_desktop_v1` metadata so post-delivery callbacks observe the delivered message id; `api_server` now exposes native HTTP health/models/capabilities plus `/v1/chat/completions` live HTTP SSE with provider deltas where the selected transport emits deltas, `/v1/responses` create/get/delete with `previous_response_id` chaining, snapshot SSE fallback, and live HTTP text-delta SSE, `/api/sessions` list/create/get/patch/delete/messages/fork/chat plus chat stream, `/v1/runs` create/status/SSE events/approval/stop, OpenAI/Hermes-style response envelopes, Bearer/x-api-key auth, multimodal content flattening, and `X-Hermes-Session-Id` desktop conversation continuity | Adapted to desktop/runtime; platform-specific external bridge daemons, full Teams SDK object lifecycle hosting, SimpleX typing lifecycle, IRC typing no-op parity, and native `api_server.py` daemon/service lifecycle remain explicit boundaries |
| Integrations/platforms | gateway platform tools, `tools/feishu_doc_tool.py`, `tools/feishu_drive_tool.py`, `tools/yuanbao_tools.py`, `tools/discord_tool.py`, `tools/homeassistant_tool.py` | `agent/integrations.rs`, Feishu docs/drive comments, Yuanbao group text via Hermes-compatible messaging gateway, Yuanbao DM/query/stickers via dedicated bridge tools, Spotify, Discord, Home Assistant, platform controls, `/platforms` Hermes capability matrix | Adapted to desktop/runtime; external gateway daemons remain explicit platform boundary |
| Microsoft Graph receive bridge | `tools/microsoft_graph_auth.py`, `tools/microsoft_graph_client.py` | `msgraph_webhook` inbound parsing through messaging gateway receive runtime, client-state/resource allow checks | Adapted to gateway receive path |

## Known Platform Adaptations

- Tirith is not auto-installed on Windows because Hermes itself marks Windows as unsupported for prebuilt Tirith binaries. SynthChat exposes diagnostics through `security_scan` and keeps built-in threat patterns plus command guards active.
- Current Tirith runtime update: `security_scan` now executes a user-configured Tirith binary when `tirithPath` / `TIRITH_BIN` points to one, preserving Hermes' `tirith check --json --non-interactive --shell posix -- <command>` invocation, exit-code verdict semantics, timeout, fail-open/fail-closed, JSON enrichment, and `.app` lookalike warning suppression. The remaining boundary is Hermes' GitHub release auto-download/cache installer; SynthChat reports that as an explicit desktop no-auto-install boundary and keeps Windows default fallback silent unless an explicit binary path is provided.
- Hermes gateway platforms are broad Python services. SynthChat implements desktop-facing platform adapters and integration tools where practical, with OS service-manager registration kept as an explicit external step.
- Yuanbao group text delivery is intentionally mapped to the Hermes platform-adapter route rather than the standalone Yuanbao bridge tool set: `send_message` accepts `yuanbao:group:<id>` and `group:<id>` when `settings.messagingGateway` enables platform `yuanbao`, then emits a gateway payload with `platform:"yuanbao"` and `chat_id:"group:<id>"`. The dedicated Yuanbao bridge tools remain aligned to Hermes' public `yuanbao_tools.py` surface for group/member lookup, DM, sticker search, and sticker send.
- `/platforms service install|start|restart|stop|uninstall` now writes Hermes-style gateway service artifacts and exposes `externalApplicationPlan` / `external_application_plan` with concrete systemd user/system, launchd, Windows scheduled-task/startup-folder, and s6 application commands. The plan keeps `appliesOsServiceManager=false` and `applyRequiresOperator=true`, so UI clients can guide operators without SynthChat silently mutating OS service managers.
- `/platforms status` now exposes machine-readable `hermesPlatforms`, `mode`, `runtimeAdapter`, `messagingGateway`, `externalDaemon`, and `capabilityMatrix` fields so UI and audits can distinguish native runtime adapters, send-only integrations, Hermes-style messaging gateway routes, and the `api_server` OpenAI-compatible HTTP/SSE boundary. `/platforms status`, `/platforms profiles`, `/platforms service`, `/platforms forensics`, `/platforms memory`, `/platforms restart`, and `/platforms stop` also persist Hermes-style desktop runtime status/intent/profile/service markers under `data_dir/platforms/`, including `gateway.pid` and `gateway.lock` records, while gateway PID/lock/state, planned-stop marker, `--replace` takeover marker, and owner-matched scoped lock cleanup are adapted to `HERMES_HOME`, `HERMES_GATEWAY_LOCK_DIR`, or desktop data-dir equivalents.
- Python `ContextVar` propagation is not directly ported because SynthChat uses explicit Rust context passing. The equivalent invariant is now recorded as `tool_approval_context` phase events.

## Evidence Status

- Strong current evidence: Hermes core `tools`, `agent`, `gateway`, `acp_adapter`, top-level CLI/server surfaces, plugin/auxiliary task surfaces, LSP diagnostics, and Bitwarden secret-source categories are now mapped in this audit; core Rust/TS validation passes; focused tests cover platform capability matrices, native `api_server` health/models/capabilities, Chat Completions provider delta SSE formatting, Responses create/get/delete with snapshot SSE and live HTTP text-delta SSE event shapes, session list/create/get/patch/delete/messages/fork/chat status and chat stream, run create/status/SSE events/approval/stop capability shape, Feishu Drive v2 comment reaction routing, Feishu comment rules/pairing access gating and pairing control commands, Feishu comment automation prompts with Hermes-style reaction/context/reply tool flow, Hermes-style desktop profile lifecycle snapshots, platform forensics snapshots with memory RSS/runtime status, gateway PID/runtime-lock persistence, gateway profile/service status boundary persistence, gateway restart request/drain-timeout persistence, gateway planned-stop marker persistence, gateway memory monitor snapshots, gateway slash-access diagnostics/gating, gateway pairing approval/rate-limit/paired-user allow integration, delivery mirror origin-session matching, ACP permissions, MCP OAuth, MCP keepalive, Bitwarden cache-backed secret resolution, Hermes-style messaging gateway targets, QQBot interaction deny/allow approval routing, WeCom callback media caching/decryption, Yuanbao raw TIM callback parsing and resource media resolution/cache, queue cancellation, and active run timeout behavior.
- Current `api_server` streaming update: `/api/sessions/{session_id}/chat/stream` now has native live HTTP SSE using Hermes-style `run.started`, `message.started`, `assistant.delta`, `assistant.completed`, `run.completed`, and `done` event names plus `X-Hermes-Session-Id`; session chat stream shape now matches the Hermes live HTTP event surface.
- Current `api_server` run streaming update: `/v1/runs` now returns HTTP 202 immediately with a pollable/subscribable run id, creates an initial queued `hermes.run`, launches the agent in the background, and binds the background agent turn to the same run id. `/v1/runs/{run_id}/events` has native live HTTP SSE transport over Hermes-style `hermes.run.event` payloads, subscribes to per-run in-process broadcasts while a run is executing, emits heartbeat comments while idle, and aborts the matching run when the live event stream disconnects before completion. `/v1/runs` accepts `stream=true` without rejecting the request. The native API server now also exposes Hermes dashboard status/action routes: `GET /api/status` returns `hermes_dashboard_status_desktop_v1` from SynthChat AppStore/runtime adapter state, `POST /api/gateway/restart` records a desktop gateway restart action through the existing `/platforms restart` control flow, `POST /api/hermes/update` reports an explicit desktop update boundary instead of running Python self-update commands, and `GET /api/actions/{name}/status` tails file-backed desktop action logs for `gateway-restart` / `hermes-update`. Dashboard session helpers are also adapted: `GET /api/sessions` now returns both OpenAI-style `{object,data,has_more}` and Hermes dashboard `{sessions,total,limit,offset}` fields, session rows include Hermes UI fields such as `is_active`, `input_tokens`, and `output_tokens`, `GET /api/sessions/{session_id}/messages` returns both `data` and `messages`, `DELETE /api/sessions/{session_id}` includes `ok:true`, `GET /api/sessions/search` scans SynthChat session titles/previews/messages and returns Hermes-style `{results}` search rows, and `GET /api/sessions/{session_id}/latest-descendant` follows `parentSessionId` fork metadata to the newest branch tip with Hermes `requested_session_id`, `session_id`, `path`, and `changed` fields. The native API server also exposes a Hermes chat-tab bridge status contract at `GET /api/chat/status` and advertises/handles the Hermes embedded dashboard WebSocket paths `/api/pty`, `/api/ws`, `/api/pub`, and `/api/events`; `/api/ws` now provides a native Hermes-style JSON-RPC compatibility surface that advertises all 74 current Hermes `tui_gateway.dispatch` methods plus desktop `ping` / `gateway.status` helpers. `prompt.submit` reuses SynthChat's native session chat runtime and emits JSON-RPC `event` frames for `run.started`, `message.started`, `assistant.delta`, `assistant.completed`, `run.completed`, and `done`, while `slash.exec` / `command.dispatch` bridge into SynthChat's native control-command registry. `shell.exec` is not executed directly inside the WebSocket handler; when the active agent allows shell, SynthChat now writes a Hermes-shaped user turn and enqueues it into the native agent queue so the command is executed through terminal/process tools with approvals, sandbox policy, tool-call UI transitions, output truncation, and run events. If shell is disabled, it returns the explicit disabled-shell boundary. Async gateway methods that may resume agent work are handled on the WebSocket path: `session.compress` calls the native `/compact` control command, `approval.respond` / `sudo.respond` / `secret.respond` resolve pending tool approvals, `rollback.restore` resumes a native AgentRun from the selected checkpoint, and `clarify.respond` records the clarification as a user turn for the native runtime. `/api/pub` and `/api/events` provide a native per-channel event bridge for sidecar publisher frames and sidebar subscribers, while `/api/pty` now keeps the socket open as a desktop status/resize/input-boundary bridge and the actual POSIX PTY child ownership remains an external runtime boundary alongside Hermes' Python slash-worker subprocess. `paste.collapse` writes Hermes-style paste files under SynthChat's data dir; `complete.path` and `complete.slash` adapt context/path and desktop slash-command completions; `browser.manage` records CDP URL state for native browser tools without launching host browsers; `insights.get` reports recent session/message counts from AppStore; `rollback.list/diff` expose SynthChat AgentCheckpointRecord summaries and event references in Hermes-shaped payloads. `spawn_tree.save/list/load` adapt Hermes TUI-written subagent tree snapshots under SynthChat's data-dir `spawn-trees` root with path-escape protection for replay/overlay consumers. `config.set` adapts common Hermes keys such as `busy`, prompt/personality, skin, cwd/workdir, and model into SynthChat config/persona state; `tools.configure` toggles config-backed desktop toolsets; `agents.list` maps managed processes into Hermes process rows; and `plugins.list` reports dashboard plugin metadata. `session.undo` removes the latest user/assistant/tool turn from persisted conversation history, `session.save` writes a Hermes-shaped conversation JSON export under the desktop sessions directory, `session.branch` creates a child conversation with copied history and parent metadata, `process.stop` maps to native managed-process stop plus active-run abort, `reload.mcp` preserves Hermes' confirm gate before refreshing desktop MCP/tool state, `reload.env` refreshes persisted desktop state, and `cli.exec` preserves Hermes' interactive/long-running block list while returning managed-process execution plans for allowed non-interactive Hermes CLI commands. SynthChat maps dashboard chat usage to its native session chat stream, run events, Kanban runtime-events APIs, `/api/ws` JSON-RPC surface, and `/api/pty` status/resize bridge while the POSIX PTY child process remains a dashboard-host boundary. Remaining run parity boundary is external Hermes daemon/process lifecycle parity rather than the native run/status/events lifecycle.
- Additional API server daemon lifecycle status refinement on 2026-06-10: `platform_adapter_status(api_server)` now exposes `hermes_api_server_daemon_lifecycle_desktop_v1`, separating the implemented native HTTP/SSE/session/run/dashboard routes from the still-external Hermes Python `api_server.py` daemon/service lifecycle. The adapter status now marks native HTTP surface, SSE streaming, run lifecycle, and dashboard routes as supported, and exposes `hermes_api_server_daemon_managed_process_plan_desktop_v1` with a SynthChat managed-process start/stop payload for `API_SERVER_ENABLED=true hermes gateway run`. It also exposes `hermes_api_server_daemon_service_manager_plan_desktop_v1`, making explicit that Hermes API server service-manager parity is a gateway service install with `API_SERVER_ENABLED=true` in the service environment across systemd, launchd, Windows scheduled task/startup fallback, or s6, while `osServiceManagerApplied` remains false. This gives the desktop process UI an explicit way to launch or plan the external Hermes gateway/API server without claiming that Python `api_server.py` is embedded or that OS service-manager changes are applied.
- Additional gateway daemon lifecycle status refinement on 2026-06-10: `/platforms status` now persists `hermes_gateway_daemon_lifecycle_desktop_v1` inside `gateway_state.json`, summarizing desktop-owned runtime status, PID/lock mirroring, restart/stop/takeover intent records, service artifact template generation, and service-state persistence. The same contract now exposes `hermes_gateway_daemon_managed_process_plan_desktop_v1` with managed-process start, replace (`hermes gateway run --replace`), and stop payloads for the external Hermes Python gateway daemon. It still explicitly marks Python `gateway/run.py` embedding and OS service-manager application (`systemd` / `launchd` / `schtasks` / `s6`) as external lifecycle boundaries, giving UI/audit clients a single machine-readable field for the remaining daemon parity gap.
- Current tool-progress update: native run event conversion now maps recorded SynthChat `ToolEvent` records into Hermes-style `tool.started`, `tool.completed`, and `tool.failed` run events with embedded `hermes.tool.progress` payloads, uses status-aware tool event ids so real-time streams do not lose completed/failed transitions after a running event is replaced, and exposes reusable `hermes.tool.progress` SSE chunks for chat/response streaming surfaces. Remaining boundary is full Hermes background-run queue lifecycle, not the basic live tool-progress event bridge.
- Current display/runtime-footer update: `/footer status|on|off` now returns a Hermes-style `hermes_runtime_footer_desktop_v1` snapshot with effective fields (`model`, `context_pct`, `cwd`), final-reply-only/trailing-footer delivery applicability, scheduled/gateway delivery coverage, and explicit exclusion from tool-progress/partial streaming deltas. `/statusbar status|on|off|toggle` now returns `hermes_display_statusbar_desktop_v1` with effective desktop display settings and a machine-readable session-context map for Hermes `HERMES_SESSION_*` names, documenting SynthChat's explicit Rust run/conversation context as the concurrency-safe equivalent of Hermes `ContextVar` session state.
- Current channel-directory/session-routing update: `/platforms status` runtime snapshots now include `hermes_channel_directory_desktop_v1` under `channelDirectory`, reporting cached Hermes `channel_directory.json` path/existence/update time, per-platform target counts, conversation-origin session discovery counts from `conversation.metadata.origin`, supported delivery target formats (`origin`, `local`, `platform`, `platform:chat_id`, `platform:chat_id:thread_id`), name-resolution availability, and delivery mirror match keys.
- Current delivery guard update: platform-bound `send_message` dispatch now mirrors Hermes `delivery.py` silence-narration anti-loop filtering before adapter delivery, dropping bare `silent`/`silence`/`no response`/`no reply`/`.`/ellipsis/speaker-muted style messages with `filteredReason=hermes_delivery_silence_narration` while leaving local delivery and media-bearing sends unfiltered; the guard is reported in the `channelDirectory.deliveryRouting.silenceNarrationFilter` status block and can be disabled with `HERMES_FILTER_SILENCE_NARRATION=false` or `SYNTHCHAT_FILTER_SILENCE_NARRATION=false`.
- Current scheduled-delivery truncation update: platform-bound scheduled job delivery now mirrors Hermes `MAX_PLATFORM_OUTPUT=4000` / `TRUNCATED_VISIBLE=3800` behavior by saving the full output under the scheduled-output history and sending a truncated platform message with a `full output saved to ...` path hint; local/SynthChat conversation delivery continues to receive the untruncated content.
- Current gateway-session lifecycle update: `/stop` now persists a Hermes-style `hermes_gateway_session_lifecycle_desktop_v1` snapshot in `conversation.metadata.hermesSessionLifecycle` with `suspended=true`, `/new` marks the created conversation as `isFreshReset=true`, agent-run timeout and startup-interrupted run normalization mark `resumePending=true` with `resumeReason` (`agent_run_timeout` or `restart_interrupted`), and `/agent-status` exposes the effective session lifecycle snapshot so UI/diagnostics can distinguish explicit session breaks, fresh resets, and resume-pending states without embedding Hermes' Python `sessions.json` store.
- Current typing-lifecycle update: `/platforms status` now exposes top-level and per-adapter `hermes_gateway_typing_lifecycle_desktop_v1` snapshots matching Hermes `_keep_typing` timing and lifecycle semantics: 2s refresh, 1.5s bounded `send_typing`, approval/clarify pause, approval/clarify resume, completion/error/interrupt stop, paused-set cleanup, per-platform support flags, and explicit external-bridge boundary notes.
- Current Mattermost typing update: `mattermost_typing` now adapts Hermes `plugins/platforms/mattermost/adapter.py::send_typing` as a native internal tool, sending `POST /api/v4/users/{bot_user_id}/typing` with the target `channel_id`, resolving `/users/me` when `botUserId` / `MATTERMOST_BOT_USER_ID` is absent, treating `stop` as natural-expiry no-op, exposing the tool in the planner prompt, and marking Mattermost as typing-capable in the Hermes typing lifecycle status.
- Current gateway-service update: `/platforms service install/start/stop/restart/status/uninstall` now persists Hermes-style desktop service lifecycle state instead of a fixed boundary-only snapshot, writes `gateway-service` artifact templates for Windows Scheduled Task scripts, systemd units, launchd plists, and s6 runtime service directories (`type`, `run`, `log/run`, `down`), and records the lifecycle event in `gateway_service.json`. OS-level/s6 registration still requires an explicit external service-manager action.
- Current platform-send post-delivery update: platform-bound `send_message` results now carry `hermes_post_delivery_callback_desktop_v1` metadata for single and chunked sends, including delivered status, transport, platform, target/thread ids, extracted message ids, chunk count, and `messageSource=send_message`, so remote delivery surfaces expose the same post-delivery evidence shape as local assistant delivery.
- Current post-delivery callback registry update: SynthChat now adapts Hermes gateway `register_post_delivery_callback` / `pop_post_delivery_callback` semantics into a desktop callback-intent registry. `pre_gateway_dispatch` hooks can return `postDeliveryCallback` / `registerPostDeliveryCallback` intents, same session/generation registrations chain in order, stale generations cannot overwrite fresher slots, callbacks are popped one-shot after delivery, shell-hook execution is isolated per callback, and fire records are persisted in `platforms/post_delivery_callbacks.json`.
- Current restart-recovery update: `/platforms restart` now writes Hermes-style `.restart_pending.json` notification markers and `.restart_failure_counts` counters mirrored into `HERMES_HOME`; active sessions are marked `resumePending=restart_interrupted`, and sessions active across three restart intents are marked suspended with `reason=restart_stuck_loop`, matching Hermes stuck-loop protection semantics.
- Current LINE adapter update: `send_message` now adapts `plugins/platforms/line/adapter.py` Push API delivery with `line:<user_or_group_or_room_id>` targets, `LINE_CHANNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN` / `LINE_HOME_CHANNEL` config, markdown-to-text conversion, 4500-character bubbles, five-message call cap, media fallback text hints, external target listing, and Hermes-style post-delivery metadata. When a LINE `replyToken` is available from payload or conversation origin metadata, SynthChat now attempts the LINE Reply API first and falls back to Push API on reply-token expiry/error. Native `messaging_gateway` receive now parses official LINE webhook `events[]`, verifies `X-Line-Signature` with HMAC-SHA256/base64 when `channelSecret` is configured, maps user/group/room identity into Hermes-style source metadata, records `replyToken` and postback payloads, dispatches normal postback display/data text through the gateway path, emits Hermes-style slow-response template buttons after `slowResponseThreshold`, captures later `send_message` or final local assistant output into the postback cache, handles Hermes `show_response` postback cache retrieval with `pending` / `ready` / `error` / `delivered` states plus Reply API delivery and Push fallback, downloads/caches image/video/audio/file message content through LINE Content API with the channel bearer token, and exposes cached media through a guarded native `/media/attachments/{platform}/{file}` HTTP route with traversal checks.
- Current Google Chat standalone sender/receive/update/attachment update: `send_message` now adapts `plugins/platforms/google_chat/adapter.py` standalone Chat REST delivery with `google_chat:spaces/<id>` and `google_chat:users/<id>` targets, strict Hermes resource-name validation, optional `thread_id=spaces/<id>/threads/<id>`, conversation-origin chat/thread/sender inference for gateway replies, `GOOGLE_CHAT_ACCESS_TOKEN` / `GOOGLE_CHAT_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` / `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` / `GOOGLE_CHAT_HOME_CHANNEL` config, service-account RS256 JWT token refresh, Application Default Credentials discovery for gcloud `application_default_credentials.json` / configured `adcCredentialsFile` authorized-user refresh tokens, 4000-character chunks, media fallback text hints, external target listing, and Hermes-style post-delivery metadata. Google Chat `media_files` now follow Hermes' native attachment path with user OAuth: per-sender tokens are selected from explicit `sender_email`, source user email, conversation `origin.userId`, `userAccessTokens` config, `GOOGLE_CHAT_USER_ACCESS_TOKENS_JSON`, or Hermes-compatible `${HERMES_HOME}/google_chat_user_tokens/<sanitized_email>.json` authorized-user token files, then fall back to legacy `userAccessToken` / `GOOGLE_CHAT_USER_ACCESS_TOKEN`; SynthChat calls `POST /upload/v1/{space}/attachments:upload?uploadType=multipart` with `multipart/related`, then `POST /v1/{space}/messages` with the returned `attachmentDataRef`; without user OAuth the prior text fallback remains. The Google Chat gateway now intercepts in-chat `/setup-files` before normal agent dispatch and adapts Hermes' per-sender user OAuth setup flow natively: status/check reports stored client-secret/token state, `start` generates a PKCE Google OAuth URL and writes `${HERMES_HOME}/google_chat_user_oauth_pending/<sanitized_email>.json`, pasted callback URLs or auth codes are exchanged through the configured Google token endpoint, and successful exchanges write Hermes-compatible `${HERMES_HOME}/google_chat_user_tokens/<sanitized_email>.json` `authorized_user` token files; `revoke` removes only the current sender's pending/token files. `google_chat_typing` adapts Hermes' visible "Hermes is thinking..." marker creation in the target thread through the same REST credentials, and `google_chat_update_message` adapts Hermes `edit_message/_patch_message` by PATCHing `/v1/{message_id}?updateMask=text` so visible typing markers/progress messages can be updated in-place without delete tombstones. Google Chat adapter status now reports configured native attachment upload, per-sender user OAuth routing, message patch/edit capability, service-account refresh, application-default-credentials refresh, configured Pub/Sub receive capability, inbound attachment metadata capability, native inbound attachment download/cache capability when Pub/Sub plus Chat REST credentials are configured, a `pubSubReceive` contract with subscription/maxMessages/REST pull request/ack request/ack policy/envelope formats/reconnect parameters/streaming-pull parity metadata, and a per-platform `pubSubRuntime` snapshot merged from the messaging gateway state with REST pull/ack transport, last Google Chat Pub/Sub event, listen URL, error, received count, and triggered count. The native inbound normalizer covers Hermes' Workspace Add-ons, native Chat API, and relay-flat MESSAGE envelope formats with BOT self-filtering, slash-command normalization, thread/source metadata, and Hermes-shaped attachment metadata: `attachmentDataRef` entries become `media_download_pending`, Google-owned `downloadUri` fallbacks become `download_uri_pending`, and non-Google download hosts become `blocked_non_google_host` to preserve Hermes' SSRF guardrail. The common gateway enrichment path now downloads `attachmentDataRef.resourceName` via `GET /v1/{resourceName}?alt=media` using Chat credentials, caches bytes under SynthChat attachments, and exposes local `media_urls` / `media_types`; Google-owned `downloadUri` fetches use the same authenticated cache path. When `messaging_gateway.platforms` includes `google_chat`, the native gateway runtime now runs a Google Pub/Sub REST pull loop against `projects/<project>/subscriptions/<subscription>:pull`, decodes base64 JSON envelopes, dispatches them through the same Google Chat normalizer/gateway enrichment/slash-access/background-run path, acknowledges received ack ids through `:acknowledge`, reports idle/pull/error events, and reconnects with bounded backoff. Remaining Google Chat boundaries are Hermes' exact Cloud Pub/Sub gRPC streaming subscriber/executor semantics and Hermes' Python adapter in-memory user-client cache; SynthChat reloads the generated token through the existing per-sender token-file path on subsequent attachment sends.
- Additional Google Chat live E2E diagnostics parity refinement on 2026-06-10: `platform_adapter_status(google_chat)` now exposes `hermes_google_chat_live_e2e_diagnostics_v1`, reporting bearer-token, service-account, Application Default Credentials, home target, native attachment upload, Pub/Sub receive, visible typing marker, and message patch readiness. It also advertises safe send/typing/PubSub smoke-test plans while explicitly marking network probes, token refresh, and Pub/Sub pulls as not performed by the diagnostic status call.
- Current Discord standalone sender update: `send_message` now adapts the Hermes Discord plugin's out-of-process forum-channel delivery path by resolving Hermes channel-directory entries with `type:"forum"` and explicit `forum` / `createForumThread` payloads into `POST /channels/{forum_channel_id}/threads` starter-thread requests, including a starter `message` payload and optional multipart attachment metadata, instead of treating forum targets as normal channel posts.
- Current Google Meet plugin update: `meet_join`, `meet_status`, `meet_transcript`, `meet_leave`, and `meet_say` now adapt `plugins/google_meet/tools.py` into SynthChat internal tools with Hermes-style explicit Meet URL validation, transcribe/realtime mode, guest/duration/headed/node fields, persistent desktop state, transcript file reads with `last`, realtime-only `meet_say` queueing, and safe leave semantics. Local desktop joins now also mirror Hermes `process_manager.py` file layout under `$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/`: `.active.json` is written on join and removed on leave, `<meeting-id>/status.json` is refreshed by status/leave, `<meeting-id>/transcript.txt` is created as the Hermes transcript mirror, and realtime `meet_say` appends JSONL rows to `<meeting-id>/say_queue.jsonl`; when `.active.json` contains a real external bot pid, `meet_status` merges `<out_dir>/status.json` telemetry, `meet_transcript` prefers `<out_dir>/transcript.txt`, `meet_say` appends to the external `<out_dir>/say_queue.jsonl`, and `meet_leave` writes an external-process stop status before clearing `.active.json`, matching Hermes `process_manager.status()` / `transcript()` / `enqueue_say()` file behavior while leaving actual process termination to the external runtime. `meet_node requirements` / `setup` now exposes `hermes_google_meet_runtime_requirements_desktop_v1`, mirroring Hermes `hermes meet setup` without installing packages: platform support, pip/playwright Chromium install commands, auth-state path/probe, realtime API-key envs, Linux/macOS audio dependency checks, and Windows remote-node fallback guidance. `meet_node audio-plan` / `realtime-plan` exposes `hermes_google_meet_realtime_audio_plan_desktop_v1`, including Linux `pactl` null-sink and virtual-source commands, macOS BlackHole check/install guidance, Chrome fake-media flags and `PULSE_SOURCE`, OpenAI Realtime WebSocket/session/update/speak/cancel frame shapes, queue/processed JSONL shape, and `speaker.pcm` sink paths. When the normal Meet tools include `node` plus `execute/live/apply:true`, SynthChat maps them to Hermes remote-node JSON-over-WebSocket RPC request types (`start_bot`, `status`, `transcript`, `stop`, `say`) and returns `hermes_google_meet_remote_node_tool_desktop_v1` with redacted request envelope, timeout handling, response-id validation, and remote response payload. Join/status/say responses also include `hermes_google_meet_runtime_contract_desktop_v1`, which mirrors Hermes process-manager state/transcript/say-queue files, the local Playwright bot subprocess command/env/log/status/transcript/say-queue plan from `process_manager.py::start`, Playwright `__hermesMeetDrain` live-caption scraping, remote node JSON-over-WebSocket request envelope and valid request types (`start_bot`, `stop`, `status`, `transcript`, `say`, `ping`), node registry path and redacted node entries, OpenAI Realtime speaker queue/audio sink metadata, and Linux PulseAudio / macOS BlackHole audio bridge expectations. `meet_node` adds native Hermes remote-node registry management over `$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/nodes.json`, can build token-redacted JSON-over-WebSocket request envelopes for external node runtimes, routes `execute/live/apply:true` through the same bounded async JSON-over-WebSocket RPC call, and exposes the `hermes meet node run` host bootstrap surface: `token` / `ensure-token` reuse or generate `$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/node_token.json`, while `host-plan` / `run` return listen URL, valid request types, redacted approve command, and equivalent Hermes CLI command without secretly launching a persistent host. The runtime contract now also includes `hermes_google_meet_live_runtime_e2e_diagnostics_v1`, which reports local platform support, storage-state readiness, registered remote nodes, realtime API-key readiness, managed/external process detection, live-runtime readiness, and safe smoke-test plans without launching Playwright or probing the network. The actual local Playwright bot subprocess host, OpenAI Realtime speaker WebSocket execution, and platform virtual-audio routing remain explicit google_meet runtime boundaries.

Warn mode appends a Hermes-style security guidance block to successful `write_file`/`patch`/mutating `skill_manage` results so the model can self-correct without blocking; `SECURITY_GUIDANCE_BLOCK=1` converts matching writes into pre-tool-call refusals, and `SECURITY_GUIDANCE_DISABLE=1` disables the adapter.
- Current dashboard-auth update: `dashboard_auth` now adapts `plugins/dashboard_auth/nous/__init__.py` as a desktop status/contract and native verification surface for Nous Portal dashboard OAuth. It reports env/config client-id resolution, `agent:{instance_id}` readiness, Portal endpoints, PKCE/S256 scope, JWT/JWKS validation expectations, no-refresh-token V1 semantics, redirect URI rules, native authorization-code token exchange, native session-cookie claim validation, env/path/live JWKS signature verification with a Hermes 300-second cache, and the explicit boundary that SynthChat desktop does not embed Hermes' Python dashboard middleware/cookie gate. - Current dashboard-plugin update: `dashboard_plugins` now adapts Hermes dashboard plugin bundles into a SynthChat desktop diagnostic/control surface. It exposes `hermes-achievements`, `example-dashboard`, and `kanban` manifest metadata, achievement API routes, the 60-achievement catalog count, scan TTL, local unlock/snapshot/checkpoint paths and counts, the example plugin hello route contract, Kanban board/task/collaboration/worker/notification/orchestration route groups, SynthChat native kanban tool mapping, native task count, Hermes `kanban.db` / worker-log paths, gateway-embedded dispatcher defaults, and deprecated standalone systemd service metadata. For `hermes-achievements`, `rescan` now performs a desktop-native scan over SynthChat conversations/messages/agent runs/tool traces, writes Hermes-layout `state.json`, `scan_snapshot.json`, and `scan_checkpoint.json`, while `reset-state`, `recent-unlocks`, and `session-badges` adapt the corresponding Hermes dashboard routes without embedding the FastAPI host. The desktop achievement evaluator now carries all 60 Hermes catalog entries, including the Hermes multi-condition achievements `full_send`, `dependency_hell_tourist`, `the_fix_was_restarting`, `ship_first_ask_later`, and `one_character_fix`, instead of preserving count via placeholders. For `kanban`, desktop-native read actions now adapt Hermes `/board`, `/stats`, `/assignees`, and `/tasks/{task_id}` dashboard routes into `hermes_kanban_dashboard_desktop_v1` payloads sourced from `AppStore.agent_kanban_tasks`, including columns, cards, comments, dependency links, assignee totals, and status totals. `kanban-events` now adapts Hermes websocket event payloads into pollable desktop cursor checkpoints sourced from task-local event arrays, and the native API server also exposes `WebSocket /api/plugins/kanban/events?since=&board=&token=` with Hermes-style `{"events":[...],"cursor":...}` text frames, RFC 6455 accept-key handling, and browser-friendly query-token auth. `kanban-runtime-events` / `kanban-runtime-checkpoint` merge queue items, AgentRun phase/tool transitions, ManagedProcess snapshots, and task events into a single Hermes-style runtime cursor stream for dashboard/frontend consumers; `list_agent_runtime_events` exposes the same stream as a Tauri command, the chat execution panel now polls it for visible queue/run/tool/task transitions, and the native API server exposes `/api/plugins/kanban/runtime-events` plus `/api/plugins/kanban/runtime-events/stream` as JSON/SSE dashboard bridges. Desktop-native write actions now adapt the core Hermes mutation routes for create/update/archive-or-delete/comment/link/unlink/bulk patch into AppStore-backed `hermes_kanban_dashboard_desktop_v1` write payloads. Worker visibility actions now adapt Hermes `/diagnostics`, `/workers/active`, `/runs/{run_id}`, `/runs/{run_id}/inspect`, `/runs/{run_id}/terminate`, `/tasks/{task_id}/log`, `/tasks/{task_id}/attachments`, `/dispatch`, `/tasks/{task_id}/reclaim`, and `/tasks/{task_id}/reassign` into desktop payloads sourced from `ManagedProcess`, `agent_runs`, task events, and metadata artifacts; the native API server now exposes `/api/plugins/kanban/board`, `/stats`, `/assignees`, `/tasks`, `/tasks/{task_id}`, `/tasks/{task_id}/comments`, `/tasks/{task_id}/attachments`, `/tasks/bulk`, `/attachments/{attachment_id}`, `/links`, `/workers/active`, `/diagnostics`, `/dispatch`, `/runs/{run_id}`, `/runs/{run_id}/inspect`, `/runs/{run_id}/terminate`, `/tasks/{task_id}/reclaim`, and `/tasks/{task_id}/reassign` as HTTP dashboard bridges with capability metadata. Attachment write/read/delete actions now adapt Hermes multipart upload/download/delete semantics through local file-backed task attachments and `attached` / `attachment_removed` events; the native API server attachment routes expose this as a JSON file-backed bridge instead of embedding FastAPI multipart/FileResponse. The native API server now also exposes Hermes-style dashboard bundle routes `GET /api/dashboard/plugins`, `GET /api/dashboard/plugins/rescan`, and `GET /dashboard-plugins/{plugin}/{file_path}`. The static asset bridge resolves plugin dashboard assets from Hermes plugin roots, rejects traversal, serves browser asset suffixes from an allowlist, and refuses Python source such as `plugin_api.py` instead of importing arbitrary FastAPI plugin code. `kanban-dispatch` still dry-runs by default for safe dashboard calls, but explicit `dryRun:false` now performs a desktop-native claim/run/process registration path, and `enqueueAgent:true` bridges claimed work into SynthChat's normal agent queue. `kanban-reclaim` and `kanban-reassign` now provide the dashboard recovery path: they release running claims, stop desktop managed worker records, abort the matched run, return the task to ready, and optionally assign a different profile after explicit reclaim. The new `dispatch_kanban_and_drain_agent_queue` Tauri command defaults to `dryRun:false` and `enqueueAgent:true`, then optionally drains the native queue through the existing async agent runtime so desktop callers can run claimed Kanban work without invoking a separate Hermes dispatcher daemon. Board/profile/orchestration actions now adapt Hermes `/boards`, `/profiles`, and `/orchestration` settings through config-backed desktop state plus persona/profile discovery. Remaining gaps are full embedded dashboard host/tab runtime, arbitrary Python `plugin_api.py` route mounting, and external Hermes daemon/process parity.
- Current dashboard-plugin management update: the native API server now adapts the Hermes dashboard plugin management routes `GET /api/dashboard/plugins/hub`, `GET /api/dashboard/plugins/api-routes`, `POST /api/dashboard/plugins/{name}/visibility`, `PUT /api/dashboard/plugin-providers`, `POST /api/dashboard/agent-plugins/install`, `POST /api/dashboard/agent-plugins/{name}/enable`, `POST /api/dashboard/agent-plugins/{name}/disable`, `POST /api/dashboard/agent-plugins/{name}/update`, and `DELETE /api/dashboard/agent-plugins/{name}`. Hub listing, hidden-plugin filtering, provider selection, and enable/disable state persist under `config.messaging_gateway.dashboardPlugins`; install/update/remove are surfaced as Hermes-shaped external `plugins_cmd` mutation boundaries instead of claiming arbitrary plugin mutation inside the desktop API server. Dashboard plugin discovery now includes trusted user plugin manifests under `$HERMES_HOME/plugins/*/dashboard/manifest.json` in addition to the bundled Hermes dashboard plugins. `GET /api/dashboard/plugins/api-routes` now mirrors Hermes `_safe_plugin_api_relpath` / `_mount_plugin_api_routes` semantics as a desktop audit surface: it enumerates dashboard manifest `api` fields, reports safe relative paths, file presence, project-plugin Python auto-import boundaries, known native API bridges, and never returns `plugin_api.py` source content. It now also statically scans safe `plugin_api.py` files for `@router.get/post/put/patch/delete/websocket` decorators without executing Python, reports the Hermes mounted paths under `/api/plugins/{name}`, and calculates native bridge coverage for adapted routes such as Kanban and achievements. Unknown `/api/plugins/{name}/...` requests now hit a generic desktop route instead of falling through to a generic unsupported endpoint: it validates the dashboard plugin name, source, safe API path, file presence, static route match, and Hermes project-plugin import rule. For trusted bundled/user plugin APIs with a matching declared route, SynthChat attempts short-lived bounded Python execution of the `plugin_api.py` endpoint with both the dashboard directory and plugin root on `sys.path`, so simple plugin-root helper imports work; the runner now writes the helper script to a temporary `.py` file before execution instead of passing the whole script via `python -c`, avoiding Windows command-line length failures as the compatibility shim grows. The runner resolves FastAPI `Depends(...)` / `Security(...)` callables through the same path/query/header/cookie/form/body parameter resolver used for endpoints, including dependency-local `Query(..., alias=...)` metadata, `SecurityScopes` injection for scoped security dependencies, route/router-level dependencies declared through `APIRouter(dependencies=...)` or decorator `dependencies=[...]`, request-local dependency result caching keyed by callable/cache key with `use_cache=False` opt-out, sync and async yield dependencies with close/aclose finalizers, request-local `BackgroundTasks` injection and post-response task execution, case-insensitive `Header(...)` lookup, `Cookie(...)` extraction from the request header, `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` `Form(...)` fields, bounded `multipart/form-data` `Form(...)` / `UploadFile` objects with `filename`, `content_type`, and async chunked `read(size)` support, URL-decoded path parameters and query values matching FastAPI/Starlette routing, list/tuple coercion for `list[T]` and optional list annotations from JSON/form/query values, repeated query parameters such as `?tags=a&tags=b`, comma-separated scalar fallback for list query values, common FastAPI parameter constraints (`ge`, `gt`, `le`, `lt`, `min_length`, `max_length`) with 422 validation errors, Pydantic v1/v2 body model construction through `model_validate`, `parse_obj`, or constructor fallback, `Field(alias=...)` JSON body aliases, embedded `Body(..., embed=True)` model payloads, a FastAPI-compatible request object for `Request`-typed or `request` / `req` parameters with `method`, `url`, `path_params`, `query_params`, `query_params.getlist(...)`, `headers`, async `json()`, and async `body()`, and real/fallback `HTTPException` status/detail/headers propagation instead of collapsing handled plugin errors into desktop execution-boundary failures. The fallback FastAPI shim now also exposes Kanban-required `status` constants such as `HTTP_202_ACCEPTED`, an importable `fastapi.status` module, `Security`, `fastapi.security.SecurityScopes`, HTTP bearer/basic, API-key header/cookie/query helpers, OAuth2 password bearer, OAuth2 authorization-code bearer, OAuth2 password-request form parsing, and `WebSocketDisconnect`, while preserving decorator-level `status_code` metadata for bounded helper responses. It also supports Starlette/FastAPI response envelopes for `FileResponse`, `JSONResponse`, `PlainTextResponse`, and generic response objects with status/media/header metadata. FileResponse envelopes now include bounded file content for small downloads as `bodyBase64` / `body_base64` with `contentLength`, `bodyTruncated`, and a 1MiB `bodyLimitBytes`, while preserving path/filename/media-type metadata. The runner also handles Hermes Kanban-style endpoint parameters such as `Query(..., alias="max")` on POST routes without treating them as body payloads. Helper/import/signature failures fall back to a `501` `hermes_dashboard_plugin_dynamic_api_boundary_v1` payload for frontends/replay tools. Remaining dashboard-plugin boundaries are the full embedded dashboard host/tab runtime, complete FastAPI dependency graph fidelity beyond the currently covered common helpers, arbitrary plugin WebSocket endpoint execution, true HTTP byte-stream response ownership for large files, and fully streaming multipart behavior for arbitrary plugin APIs, plus external Hermes daemon/process parity.

- Additional focused validation for Hermes chat-tab background prompt and attachment/drop parity passed on 2026-06-09 and was extended on 2026-06-10 for extracted clipboard payloads plus Windows/Linux/macOS system clipboard image extraction: `prompt.background`, `clipboard.paste`, `image.attach`, `image.detach`, and `input.detect_drop` are now part of the native `/api/ws` JSON-RPC subset. `prompt.background` appends a Hermes-tagged user message and enqueues it into SynthChat's native agent queue, returning Hermes-style `task_id` while keeping actual execution on the queue drain/runtime path rather than spawning a Python daemon thread. `image.attach` and `input.detect_drop` resolve absolute, cwd-relative, and quoted drag/drop paths, persist session attachment state under conversation metadata `chatTabAttachments`, return Hermes-style `attached` / `matched` / `path` / `count` / `text` fields, and include image MIME metadata for supported image extensions. `image.detach` removes matching persisted attachment paths. `clipboard.paste` now accepts frontend/Tauri-extracted image paths, file arrays, image data URLs, or base64 image payloads and attaches them through the same native session metadata path; when no payload is supplied on Windows, SynthChat tries the Hermes-style PowerShell WinForms/Get-Clipboard image backends, saves extracted bitmap images as PNG under the app data directory, and also supports PowerShell FileDropList image path extraction before attaching natively. On Linux it now follows Hermes' backend order more closely: WSL first tries `powershell.exe` with the same PowerShell scripts, then Wayland `wl-paste --type image/png/jpeg/bmp/gif/webp`, then X11 `xclip -selection clipboard -t image/png -o`; Wayland non-PNG images are normalized to PNG through local Pillow (`python`/`python3`) or ImageMagick `convert` before attachment, matching Hermes' `save_clipboard_image(dest)` PNG-output contract. On macOS it now supports Hermes' primary `pngpaste <dest>` path and `osascript` PNGf fallback, saving directly into the chat-tab clipboard cache before attaching. - Additional focused validation for Hermes context-engine compression parity passed on 2026-06-09: when `SYNTHCHAT_CONTEXT_ENGINE` or `HERMES_CONTEXT_ENGINE` names a non-default engine under `plugins/context_engine/<name>`, manual `/compact`, automatic LLM preflight context-budget compaction, and final post-turn compression now call that engine's Python `compress(messages, current_tokens=..., focus_topic=...)` through the bounded context-engine bridge. Preflight calls `should_compress_preflight(messages)` when implemented, treating `false` as a skip only while the rough token estimate remains below the configured budget. Successful LLM calls forward Hermes-style usage buckets to `update_from_response(usage)`, final assistant delivery can call `should_compress(prompt_tokens)` before post-turn compression, and SynthChat session start/end/reset dispatches `on_session_start(session_id, **kwargs)`, `on_session_end(session_id, messages)`, and `on_session_reset()` to the selected engine. If the engine is missing or fails, these paths fall back to the native SynthChat summary path. The remaining boundary is Hermes' long-lived in-process engine object model rather than bounded helper subprocesses.

The focused dynamic route test now covers a sync yield dependency that writes durable state in its finalizer and verifies the state on a follow-up request. - Additional focused validation for Hermes dashboard plugin dependency-overrides parity passed on 2026-06-10: the bounded Python dashboard plugin API runner now detects FastAPI-style `dependency_overrides` mappings from module-level `app`, `router.dependency_overrides_provider`, or router objects, and replaces the original dependency callable before the existing route/parameter/Security dependency resolver runs. - Additional focused validation for Hermes dashboard plugin HTTP middleware parity passed on 2026-06-10: the bounded Python dashboard plugin API runner now executes simple FastAPI `@app.middleware("http")` / dispatch middleware around matched trusted plugin routes, including request.app.state mutation before `call_next(...)` and response header mutation after route execution. Plain dict endpoints keep their existing direct JSON shape when middleware does not add response metadata; middleware-mutated responses use the existing `hermes_dashboard_plugin_response_v1` envelope so native API consumers can observe headers/status. This narrows the full FastAPI app-process boundary while leaving lifespan events, cross-request in-process state, and arbitrary long-lived ASGI behavior outside the bounded helper model.

- Additional focused validation for Hermes ByteRover CLI execution parity passed on 2026-06-11: `brv_query`, `brv_curate`, and `brv_status` are now registered as native internal tools, default to no-execute planning/diagnostics, require `confirmByteRoverLive:true` for live CLI execution, run inside `$HERMES_HOME/byterover`, preserve Hermes `query --`, `curate --`, and `status` argument shapes, enforce bounded query/status/curate timeouts, and return stdout/stderr/exit metadata for UI/tool traces. The focused memory-provider test covers a fake local brv CLI so verification does not depend on a real ByteRover installation or cloud sync.

- Additional focused validation for Hermes OpenViking local-file and local-directory resource upload parity passed on 2026-06-12: `viking_add_resource` confirmed-live execution now detects local file paths and `file://` URIs, reads local files directly, zips local directories into a temp archive while skipping symlinks and preserving relative archive paths, sends multipart `file` to `/api/v1/resources/temp_upload` with tenant/auth headers, extracts `temp_file_id`, and then calls `/api/v1/resources` with `temp_file_id` plus the original file/directory `source_name` instead of a remote `path`, matching Hermes `upload_temp_file(...)` + resource-create flow for local resources. Remaining OpenViking boundaries are live session-hook E2E and external server/runtime lifecycle proof.

- Additional focused validation for Hermes dashboard plugin OAuth security helper parity passed on 2026-06-12: the bounded FastAPI fallback runner now exposes `OAuth2AuthorizationCodeBearer`, `OAuth2PasswordRequestForm`, `OAuth2PasswordRequestFormStrict`, and `OpenIdConnect` from `fastapi.security` / `fastapi.security.oauth2`, preserves bearer-token extraction for authorization-code and OpenID Connect security dependencies, parses `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` password-request forms into `grant_type`, `username`, `password`, `scope`/`scopes`, `client_id`, and `client_secret`, and advertises `oauth2AuthorizationCodeBearerSecurity`, `oauth2PasswordRequestForm`, `oauth2PasswordRequestFormStrict`, and `openIdConnectSecurity` in the dynamic runner contract. Remaining dashboard-plugin security gaps are uncommon helper classes and full long-lived FastAPI dependency graph fidelity rather than the common OAuth2/dashboard auth forms.

- Additional focused validation for Hermes dashboard plugin forwarded-prefix/root-path parity passed on 2026-06-12: the bounded FastAPI/Starlette fallback request now derives `request.scope["root_path"]` and `request.base_url` from `X-Forwarded-Prefix` while preserving Starlette's behavior that `request.url` remains the request path without the root prefix. `request.url_for(...)` and `URLPath.make_absolute_url(request.base_url)` now generate prefixed absolute URLs, and the dynamic runner contract advertises `requestForwardedPrefixRootPath` / `request_forwarded_prefix_root_path`.

- Additional focused validation for Hermes dashboard plugin ASGI server-scope parity passed on 2026-06-12: the bounded FastAPI/Starlette fallback request now synthesizes `request.scope["server"]` from the request host, forwarded port, and scheme alongside the existing client/scheme/path/header scope fields. This gives trusted dashboard plugin routes the same host/port tuple shape expected from ASGI `Request.scope` without requiring a long-lived embedded FastAPI server.

- Additional focused validation for Hermes dashboard plugin ASGI path-params scope parity passed on 2026-06-12: the bounded FastAPI/Starlette fallback request now mirrors decoded route parameters into `request.scope["path_params"]` in addition to `request.path_params`, so trusted dashboard plugin routes that inspect the raw ASGI scope see the same route parameter map shape as Starlette/FastAPI.

- Additional focused validation for Hermes dashboard plugin ASGI route metadata scope parity passed on 2026-06-12: the bounded FastAPI/Starlette dashboard plugin runner now injects Starlette/FastAPI-style `request.scope["endpoint"]`, `request.scope["route"]`, `request.scope["router"]`, and `request.scope["app"]` metadata for matched plugin API routes. Real FastAPI route objects are preserved when available, while fallback route dictionaries receive lightweight `APIRoute` / `APIRouter` / `FastAPI` shims with endpoint name, route name, `path`, and `path_format` fields. The dynamic runner contract now advertises `requestRouteScope` / `request_route_scope`, `requestEndpointScope` / `request_endpoint_scope`, and `requestAppRouterScope` / `request_app_router_scope`. This improves common ASGI inspection parity without claiming full long-lived FastAPI application/router lifecycle parity.

- Additional focused validation for Hermes dashboard plugin routing import parity passed on 2026-06-12: the bounded dashboard plugin fallback now exposes lightweight `starlette.routing.Route`, `Router`, `Mount`, `WebSocketRoute` and `fastapi.routing.APIRoute`, `APIRouter`, `APIWebSocketRoute` import surfaces with common `path`, `path_format`, `name`, `methods`, and nested `routes` metadata. The dynamic runner contract now advertises `starletteRoutingImports` / `starlette_routing_imports` and `fastApiRoutingImports` / `fastapi_routing_imports`. This covers common plugin import/object-inspection compatibility while leaving full Starlette router matching, lifespan, and arbitrary WebSocket handler execution in the existing long-lived dashboard host boundary.

- Additional dashboard plugin route-audit contract refinement on 2026-06-12: `/api/dashboard/plugins/api-routes` now reports trusted bundled/user plugin API files with `status: bounded_dynamic_http_runner` plus `boundedDynamicHttpRunner` / `bounded_dynamic_http_runner` instead of the stale `dynamic_python_boundary` wording. The safety contract still reports project plugin Python auto-import as disabled, matching Hermes' GHSA-5qr3-c538-wm9j boundary, while making the frontend-visible audit agree with the bounded dynamic HTTP execution path already used for matching trusted plugin routes.

- Additional Hermes chat-tab `skills.manage install` plan parity on 2026-06-12: remote skill identifiers no longer return a hard blocked response from the native `/api/ws` compatibility surface. They now produce `hermes_tui_skills_install_plan_desktop_v1` with the exact `hermes skills install <identifier>` CLI/process command, optional `--force` / `--category` shaping, confirmation metadata, status follow-up, and local desktop alternatives for `install_file`, `install_content`, and `add_tap`. Local SKILL.md path/content installs remain desktop-native through `skill_manage`; remote hub fetching still stays an explicit external Hermes CLI/process boundary rather than an unreviewed network download inside the JSON-RPC dispatcher.

- Additional Hermes chat-tab `skills.manage` action coverage on 2026-06-12: the native `/api/ws` compatibility surface now accepts Hermes hub actions for `check` / `check_updates`, `audit`, `audit_log`, `list_installs` / provenance, `usage`, `list_taps`, Hermes-style `tap list`, and `curator_status`, routing them through SynthChat's existing `skill_manage` provenance, audit, tap, usage, and curator state surfaces. This keeps frontend queue/progress/approval views aligned with the exact Hermes command a user would run while retaining the existing explicit external-CLI boundary for remote hub mutations.

- Additional Hermes chat `/auth login qwen-oauth` parity on 2026-06-12: the native auth control command no longer reports Qwen OAuth login/poll as an unknown implementation. It now mirrors Hermes' `auth add qwen-oauth` behavior as an explicit external Qwen CLI plan (`qwen auth qwen-oauth` / `hermes auth add qwen-oauth`) and points the user to `/auth refresh qwen-oauth` after the CLI writes credentials, while `/auth poll qwen-oauth` reports the same no-browser-polling boundary. This matches the dashboard OAuth provider route where Qwen remains an external CLI provider rather than a SynthChat-hosted PKCE/device-code flow.

- Additional Hermes chat `/auth login anthropic` parity on 2026-06-12: the native auth control command now exposes the same Anthropic PKCE start/poll shape as Hermes `auth add anthropic` and the existing dashboard OAuth route. `/auth login anthropic` generates the Claude authorize URL, state, code verifier, code challenge, and console callback redirect without printing secrets; `/auth poll anthropic <callback_or_code> <state> <code_verifier>` completes through the existing Anthropic token-exchange/persistence path that writes Hermes-compatible `.anthropic_oauth.json` plus credential-pool entries.

- Additional Hermes chat `/auth refresh anthropic` parity on 2026-06-12: Anthropic PKCE credentials can now be refreshed from SynthChat's native auth control command. The refresh path reads Hermes-compatible `.anthropic_oauth.json`, posts a JSON `refresh_token` grant to the Anthropic console token endpoint with the Hermes client id, preserves rotated or existing `refreshToken`, recomputes `expiresAt`, and reuses the existing persistence path so `.anthropic_oauth.json` and the `credential_pool.anthropic` dashboard PKCE entry stay in sync. Missing credentials now direct the user to `/auth login anthropic` instead of reporting an unimplemented refresh command.

- Additional Hermes chat `/auth login claude-code` parity on 2026-06-12: the native auth control command now mirrors the Hermes dashboard `claude-code` provider as an explicit external Claude Code CLI setup-token boundary instead of reporting an unknown implementation. `/auth login claude-code` returns the `claude setup-token` plan, the related Hermes Anthropic credential-store handoff, and `/auth refresh anthropic` follow-up; `/auth poll claude-code` and `/auth refresh claude-code` now explain the no-browser-polling / external-token-owner boundary.

- Additional Hermes dashboard `claude-code` provider status parity on 2026-06-12: the OAuth provider catalog no longer reports Claude Code as permanently missing. - Additional Hermes dashboard update action refinement on 2026-06-12: `POST /api/hermes/update` no longer reports a failed `desktop_update_boundary`. It now records a successful dashboard action plan, returns the Hermes `hermes update` command, confirmation metadata, and a managed-process start payload so the desktop frontend/agent can run the update through explicit approvals, logs, and stop controls. The async internal-tool dispatchers still perform the actual start/stop/restart through SynthChat's managed-process path and return `managed_process_started` / `managed_process_stopped`, while frontend callers no longer see an execution request mislabeled as a boundary failure.

- Additional Hermes chat-tab async-control boundary refinement on 2026-06-12: synchronous `/api/ws` JSON-RPC helper calls for async-only methods such as `voice.tts`, `voice.record`, `rollback.restore`, and `session.compress` now return `hermes_tui_gateway_async_control_required_desktop_v1` with `status: async_control_required`, `ok:true`, camel/snake `requiresWebSocket`, and `/api/ws` async-control route metadata. This keeps browser/front-end callers from treating a WebSocket-only method hint as a generic failed tool response while preserving the native async dispatcher for actual execution.

Actual prompt/slash/command execution remains on the WebSocket async path so streaming events, approvals, and tool UI transitions stay visible.

- Additional Hermes chat-tab async WebSocket dispatch consistency on 2026-06-12: the `/api/ws` gateway now uses a shared async-control method classifier for the WebSocket loop and the prompt-submit wrapper, so async-only methods such as `rollback.restore` cannot drift between the outer dispatch list and the inner async dispatcher. Focused coverage now proves `rollback.restore` is classified as async-control instead of being misrouted through the prompt-submit stream path.

- Additional Hermes chat-tab `rollback.restore` runtime refinement on 2026-06-12: the async `/api/ws` rollback restore path no longer re-enters the full agent loop from inside the JSON-RPC tool handler. It now records the selected `AgentCheckpointRecord`, appends a rollback-restore continuation message, enqueues that continuation into SynthChat's native agent queue, marks the source run `rollback_restore_queued`, and returns the queue item plus resume prompt in `hermes_tui_rollback_restore_desktop_v1`. This preserves Hermes' checkpoint-restore intent while keeping desktop queue progress, cancellation, timeout, and tool UI transitions visible, and avoids the direct `resume_agent_run` stack-overflow risk observed in focused chat-tab coverage.
